ui.deckReset: The deck has been gathered and shuffled.
ui.joinAria: Enter a room code to join the table
ui.newRoom: A new room has been created.
ui.pasteJoin: Paste a code or link to join
ui.alreadyInRoom: You are already at this table.
ui.invalidCode: No valid room code found on the clipboard.
ui.connLost: Connection lost. Reconnecting. Your moves are saved and will sync shortly.
ui.connRestored: Back online. Your moves are now in sync.

diag.title: Connection Self-Test
diag.subtitle: Checks if live multiplayer synchronization is working properly.
diag.verdictOk: Everything looks good. Live multiplayer is working perfectly.
diag.verdictBad: Multiplayer synchronization is currently failing. Please check the notes below.
diag.error: The test could not be completed. Please try again.
diag.hint_config-missing: Ensure SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are set in your hosting environment, then redeploy. You can verify this by visiting /api/config in your browser.
diag.hint_url-bad: The project address must be exactly https://your-project.supabase.co without any extra paths. You can find this in your Supabase dashboard.
diag.hint_rest-failed: Could not reach the project or the public key was rejected. Double-check your URL and key, and ensure your Supabase project is active.
diag.hint_realtime-failed: The project is reachable, but the live socket failed to connect. Ensure Realtime is enabled in your Supabase dashboard and check your firewall settings.

kick.title: Remove Player
kick.body: Are you sure you want to remove {name} from the table? They will be moved to a new, empty room.
kick.done: {name} has been removed from the table.
kick.kicked: You have been removed from the table by the host.

welcome.title: Welcome to the Table
welcome.guidePointer: Use the Guide in the top right corner to learn how to play step by step. You can copy the invite link from the Menu to bring your friends here.
desktopItems: Click and drag to move a card. Hold Ctrl while dragging to move an entire pile.
Scroll your mouse wheel to flip a single card. Right-click to flip a whole pile.
Press G to gather cards, M to shuffle, and D to tidy up your personal area.
The frosted glass area at the bottom of the screen is your private hand. Only you can see cards placed there.
Press V to change your perspective and view the table from a different angle.
touchItems: Use your finger to drag and move a card.
Long-press any card to open a menu with options to flip, rotate, shuffle, and more.
The frosted glass area at the bottom of your screen is your private hand. Keep your secrets there.
welcome.longPress: Pro tip: Long-press a card on mobile to open its action menu.

shortcutsList.title: Shortcuts
shortcutsList.subtitle: Most controls apply to the card directly under your cursor. A few commands apply to your private hand. Clicking on empty space does nothing.

items.desc: Move the card under your cursor.
Flip the single card under your cursor.
Flip the entire stack under your cursor.
Move the entire stack under your cursor.
Rotate the card or pile 90 degrees sideways. This also neatly squares up messy piles.
Gather loose cards into a neat stack.
Shuffle the stack under your cursor.
Rotate your view of the table. Press it again to cycle through seats. This only changes what you see and is great for reading cards clearly.
Tidy up your private hand. This action groups matching cards, sorts them by type, and centers them perfectly in your zone. Your opponents will just see your hidden cards slide neatly into place.
Open the quick action menu for flipping, rotating, shuffling, or reading card details.
Move a card across the table.

shortcutsList.mobileNote: Since touchscreens do not have keyboards, simply long-press any card to access its controls. You can also long-press one of your private cards to tidy up your entire hand automatically.

leaveConfirm.title: Leave the Room?
leaveConfirm.body: If you leave, you will be placed into a brand new room where you are the host. Any cards you leave behind will become public for the remaining players.

roomFull.title: Room is Full
roomFull.body: All four seats at this table are currently taken. You can create your own room and invite others to play.

joinByCode.title: Join a Room
joinByCode.body: Enter a room code or paste an invite link below to join an existing game. This will remove you from your current table.
joinByCode.label: Room Code or Link

settings.title: Settings
settings.resetDefaultsHint: Reset all audio sliders to their recommended default levels.

modePicker.title: Choose a Game
modePicker.subtitle: Switch any time. The table reloads with the new game's deck, art, and rules; your language and audio settings stay.

zan.name: ZAN: Perfect Doubt
zan.desc: A fast four-player bluffing game. Slide a card, name a suit, and hope they believe you. Easy to learn, all about reading faces.

vaerum.name: Vaerum: Heirs of the Ether
vaerum.desc: A deep 2-4 player duel of seals, spells, and servants, racing to declare Ascension and survive the Trial.

support.noUrl: The support page is currently unavailable. Please check back later.
support.thanks: Thank you for keeping the table alive.

entries.title: Bolder Logos and a Smoother Table
items: Redrew every logo bolder so the browser tab icon and in-app marks stay crisp and readable even at tiny sizes.
Tightened the shadow a picked-up card casts so it never spills across the table onto the seat opposite, in any browser.
Made the table lighter and smoother under load: cards are only promoted to their own layer while actually moving, so a full table stutters less.

entries.title: Interaction and Visual Fixes
items: The loading screen now shows the logo of the game you are opening, and the splash mark was redrawn to stay crisp.

Fixed a false connection warning that could flash on a page refresh; you are only told the connection dropped after it was genuinely lost, and the notice was restyled into a tidy bubble that no longer overlaps the menu.
The card info panel shows the card's centred symbol clearly again, and a card with no art yet shows the game's emblem behind it.
The Settings restore-defaults button is now greyed out when everything is already at its default and lights up the moment you change a slider.
The paste button no longer pops the browser's own paste menu; the join field is focused so you can paste straight away.
In fullscreen, a card you pick up no longer casts a stray shadow over the seat across the table.

entries.title: Smoother Sound and a Polished Switcher
items: Music now fades smoothly when you switch games instead of cutting, and a game that ships no music plays nothing at all, no hum or hiss.
Sound effects fade cleanly at their tail, so no click or crackle at the end of a flip, shuffle, or place.
Refined the Change Game panel to match the table's look, with crisper game logos that stay sharp even at tiny sizes.
Every game now shows a clean placeholder for any card whose art is not in yet, so a work-in-progress deck always reads clearly.

entries.title: Duskhall: One Table, Many Games
items: Duskhall is now a platform that hosts several games on the same table. Open the menu and choose Change Game to switch between them, with a loading screen while the new deck, art, and rules load.
Added ZAN: Perfect Doubt, a fast four-player bluffing game of claims and challenges. It opens by default for first-time visitors.
Vaerum: Heirs of the Ether is now one of the games in Duskhall, unchanged in its rules and deck.
Each game keeps its own logo, rulebook, guide, card art, and share preview. Your language and audio settings carry across every game.

feedback.title: Share Your Feedback
feedback.intro: Did you spot a bug? Do you have an idea to make the table better? Let us know.
feedback.none: Feedback channels are not set up yet. Please check back later.

legalDoc.title: About & Legal
legalDoc.subtitle: Understanding Duskhall and your data privacy.
legalDoc.intro: Duskhall is a free, browser-based platform of digital card games for friends. It hosts several games on one shared table. This page explains how the table works, how your privacy is protected, and the terms of use, for the platform as a whole rather than any single game.

sections.title: How the Shared Table Works
body: Duskhall provides a shared digital sandbox. You and your friends interact with the cards in real time. The system does not enforce game rules automatically. You play exactly as you would at a physical table, following each game's rulebook together.

Every game runs on the same table engine. Only the deck, the art, the branding, and the rulebook change from one game to another. You can switch games from the menu, and the table reloads with the new game while your language and audio settings stay.

Each player gets a private zone at the bottom of their screen. Cards dropped here are hidden from everyone else. Dragging a card out of this zone makes it public. This works seamlessly across all devices.

Basic controls are intuitive. Drag to move, scroll to flip, and use simple keyboard keys like G to gather and M to shuffle. You can find the complete list in the Shortcuts menu. The host can open the interactive Guide to walk new players through a game, and can reset the table at any time.

sections.title: About Duskhall
body: Duskhall is a collection of card games played in your browser using lightweight, real-time technology. There are no central game servers storing your matches; the table simply relays moves between the people in a room.
Each game is designed for a small group of friends and runs entirely in the browser. New games can be added to the platform over time, each with its own deck, rules, and identity.
Duskhall is crafted by an independent designer and is completely free to play.

sections.title: Privacy Matters
body: Duskhall is designed to respect your digital privacy completely.
No Accounts Required: You will never be asked for an email, password, or username.
Zero Tracking: There are no analytics, advertising networks, or hidden trackers. Your sessions are yours alone.
Local Storage: Your volume preferences, language, and which game you last played are saved only on your own device.
Transient Data: Your mouse movements and card actions are broadcast to your room in real time and are immediately discarded. Nothing is permanently stored.
Secure Rooms: Anyone with your specific room link can join your table. Keep your link safe and share it only with trusted friends.

sections.title: Terms of Use
body: Duskhall is provided for personal entertainment 'as is', without any warranties. The service may undergo changes or downtime without notice.
Please play respectfully. Do not attempt to exploit the service or disrupt games belonging to other people.
The creator of Duskhall holds no liability for any issues arising from the use of this service.
By playing, you agree to these simple terms.

sections.title: Copyright Notice
body: The name Duskhall, the names of its games, their card artwork, text, rulebooks, and visual assets are original works protected by copyright law.

You are highly encouraged to stream the games, record videos, and share them with friends. However, you may not copy, sell, or distribute the assets commercially without explicit written consent from the author.
© 2026 The Duskhall Designer. All rights reserved.

resetDeckConfirm.title: Reset the Deck?
resetDeckConfirm.body: This will aggressively gather every single card on the table, shuffle them together, and place them face down in the center. This affects all players instantly.

restartGameConfirm.title: Restart the Guide?
restartGameConfirm.body: This resets the tutorial guide back to step one for everyone. Your actual cards will not be touched.
